title: Nursery Rhyme. XLVI. Tales.
content:
[The tale of Simple Simon forms one of the chap-books, but the following verses are those generally sung in the nursery.]

        Simple Simon met a pieman
            Going to the fair;
        Says Simple Simon to the pieman,
            "Let me taste your ware."

        Says the pieman to Simple Simon,
            "Show me first your penny."
        Says Simple Simon to the pieman,
            "Indeed I have not any."

        Simple Simon went a fishing
            For to catch a whale:
        All the water he had got
            Was in his mother's pail.
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. XLVI. Tales." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
